Declaration Of Richard J Simmons In Support Of Request For Court Judgment On Default
Off Calendar May-31-2024 Off Calendar

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF PROCESS
Off Calendar May-31-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF PROCESS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to quash is granted.

A party may file motion to quash in conjunction with a motion to vacate default under CCP 473 per CCP 418.10(d) ["No default may be entered against the defendant before expiration of his or her time to plead, and no motion under this section, or under Section 473 or 473.5 when joined with a motion under this section, or application to the court or stipulation of the parties for an extension of the time to plead, shall be deemed a general appearance by the defendant." (emphasis added).]

As explained in the companion motion to vacate, plaintiff fails to show proper substitute service. Plaintiff has the burden to demonstrate jurisdiction including proper service. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440).

While service of process statutes are liberally construed where there is actual notice, defendant did not have actual notice of this lawsuit until after the default was entered. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 411; Doyka Decl. in support of motion to quash, par. 6; Doyka Decl. in support of motion to set aside, par. 5.) There was no substantial compliance with the substitute service statute (CCP 415.20(b)) since plaintiff served the wrong address. (Zirbes v. Stratton (1986) 187 Cal.App.3d 1407, 1416 [concluding that substitute service at defendant's parent's house was insufficient under former strict standard for service statutes].)

The court did not consider defendant's reply evidence. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 ["The general rule of motion practice is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE CLERK'S ENTRY OF DEFAULT
Off Calendar May-31-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE CLERK'S ENTRY OF DEFAULT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on December 26, 2023, is granted. "[T]he law favors disposing of cases on their merits." (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.)

Plaintiff purportedly substitute served defendant at 69 Colgate Drive, Rancho Mirage, California. (Simons Decl., Ex. 14.) The weight of the evidence demonstrates that the Rancho Mirage property is not defendant's "dwelling house, usual place of abode, [or] usual place of business. (CCP 415.20(b).) Defendant has a New York driver's license and is actively involved in New York real estate. (Doyka Decl. in support of motion to quash, par. 2.) Defendant's mother resides at the Rancho Mirage property and it is not defendant's usual abode or usual place of business. (Id. at pars. 3-5.) Plaintiff notes that defendant's filing with the California Department of Real Estate lists the Rancho Mirage property as his "main office." (Simons Decl., Ex. 11.) But defendant explains that he uses his mother's address merely for convenience with regard to his license. (Doyka Decl. in support of motion to quash, par. 4.) The default is vacated as void since plaintiff failed to effectuate proper service of defendant. (CCP 473.)

The court did not consider defendant's reply evidence. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1536-1537 ["The general rule of motion practice is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
